text.title: Video Prompt Engineering — Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns in video prompt engineering.

failure-mode.description: Terse prompt — "dog running"; output wobbly, off-model, inconsistent across regenerations.
failure-mode.detector: prompt length <30 chars OR missing ≥3 SASSCL fields
failure-mode.repair: Apply SASSCL formula; fill all 6 fields. Expect 2-3× quality lift.

failure-mode.description: Same prompt across providers — terms that work on Runway fail on Luma; output mediocre everywhere.
failure-mode.detector: provider_overlay missing OR identical across providers
failure-mode.repair: Per-provider overlay: Runway camera vocab, Luma prose, Sora multi-stage, Veo audio.

failure-mode.description: No eval gate — promoted prompt produces 1/5 acceptable in production; high regen rate.
failure-mode.detector: eval block missing OR sample_size <5 OR threshold <0.8
failure-mode.repair: Generate 5 samples per template; rate; promote only ≥4/5.

failure-mode.description: Prompt overflow — Runway truncates at 500 chars; later parts ignored; loss of brand style.
failure-mode.repair: Trim prompt; prioritise critical fields; move locked style to per-provider system instruction if supported.

failure-mode.description: Brand drift — Style / Camera / Lighting varies between generations; brand visual identity fragmented.
failure-mode.detector: style or camera or lighting changes between prompts in same brand
failure-mode.repair: Lock 3 fields at template level; restrict variation to Subject + Action only.
